## How Much Is Health Insurance in The Villages FL 2026?

URL: https://localblog.trentadvisors.com/blog/how-much-is-health-insurance-in-the-villages-fl-2026/

Skip to main content &#8592; Back to Blog Trent Advisors Contact Trent Advisors &#8594; Home / Blog / How Much Is Health Insurance in The Villages FL 2026? SEO How Much Is Health Insurance in The Villages FL 2026? &#10003; Updated today By Trent Advisors &#183; The Villages, FL &#183; 12 min read &#183; 2026-05-21 &#183; Last verified 2026-05-21 Last reviewed 2026-05-21 by Trent Advisors Quick Facts Call (407) 754-6090 Area The Villages, FL + surrounding cities Pricing $$ — call for quote Serving The Villages, FL and surrounding cities Table of Contents What does a 2026 Medicare Advantage plan cost in The Villages, FL? How much is a Medigap Plan G policy in 2026? How much does ACA marketplace health insurance cost for pre-retirees? What is the 2026 Medicare Part B premium and deductible? Why is health insurance often cheaper in The Villages than other Florida areas? Where do most retirees here get tripped up on cost? When can residents change health insurance plans in 2026? How much do prescription drugs cost under Medicare in 2026? How does Medigap vs Medicare Advantage compare on total cost? What credentials should a legitimate Florida health insurance agent have? What does the enrollment process look like? Who pays IRMAA surcharges and how can they be reduced? What do the public data say about coverage here? Checklist: How to prepare for your 2026 enrollment Myths vs facts Red flags to watch for Related searches Authoritative sources for this industry THE VILLAGES &mdash; May 21, 2026 &mdash; Published by Trent Advisors How Much Does Health Insurance Cost in The Villages, FL in 2026? A Pricing Breakdown TL;DR: In 2026, health insurance in The Villages, FL ranges from $0 to $180 per month for Medicare Advantage plans, $150 to $350 for Medicare Supplement Plan G, and $450 to $1,100 for ACA marketplace plans for adults aged 55-64 before subsidies. Trent Advisors (an insurance agency in The Villages, FL) helps residents compare carriers across Sumter, Lake, and Marion counties. # Key takeaways Medicare Advantage plans in Sumter County start at $0/month for 2026. Medigap Plan G averages $150-$350/month for new enrollees aged 65. ACA marketplace plans cost $450-$1,100/month before subsidies. Part B base premium is $185.00/month in 2026 (CMS). Drug coverage now caps out-of-pocket at $2,100 under Part D in 2026. Trent Advisors, an independent insurance agency serving The Villages, FL, helps retirees compare 2026 Medicare Advantage, Medigap, and ACA marketplace plans across Sumter, Lake, and Marion counties — where average Medicare Advantage premiums start at $0 and Medigap Plan G ranges from $150 to $350 per month. The Villages (a master-planned retirement community spanning Sumter, Lake, and Marion counties, ZIPs 32159, 32162, 32163) has a median age of 73.3 — among the oldest in the United States — per the U.S. Census Bureau American Community Survey (source: census.gov ). That demographic concentration shapes which carriers compete here and keeps Medicare Advantage premiums lower than the Florida statewide average. Florida's subtropical climate also drives higher utilization of cardiology, orthopedics, and dermatology — categories worth checking when comparing 2026 plan networks. What does a 2026 Medicare Advantage plan cost in The Villages, FL? Medicare Advantage is a private health plan that replaces Original Medicare Parts A and B and usually bundles Part D drug coverage. Most 2026 Medicare Advantage plans in Sumter County carry a $0 monthly premium, though enrollees still pay the standard Part B premium of $185.00/month set by CMS. According to Trent Advisors, the 2026 Medicare Advantage market in The Villages includes plans from Humana, UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, and Florida Blue. Premiums for MA-PD plans (Medicare Advantage with prescription drug coverage) range from $0 to $89/month depending on benefits. Maximum out-of-pocket limits sit between $4,500 and $8,850 in-network for 2026. Plans serving the Brownwood, Lake Sumter Landing, and Spanish Springs town squares typically include dental allowances of $1,000-$3,000 annually and over-the-counter benefits of $25-$100 monthly. Always verify your specific physicians at UF Health The Villages and Advent Health Waterman remain in-network before enrolling. How much is a Medigap Plan G policy in 2026? A Medigap Plan G policy is standardized supplemental insurance that covers most cost-sharing left by Original Medicare except the annual Part B deductible. Medigap Plan G in The Villages, FL costs $150 to $350 per month in 2026 for a 65-year-old non-tobacco user, depending on carrier and underwriting. 2026 Medigap Plan G Industry-Average Monthly Premium Ranges — Florida (source: NAIC and Florida OIR rate filings) Age Female (non-tobacco) Male (non-tobacco) 65 $150-$220 $165-$245 70 $180-$265 $200-$290 75 $215-$310 $235-$340 80 $255-$360 $280-$395 Experts at Trent Advisors recommend comparing at least three carriers because Florida uses attained-age rating, meaning premiums increase as you age. High-Deductible Plan G is also available with premiums of $45-$80/month and a 2026 deductible of $2,870 (source: medicare.gov ). Learn more: Health Insurance The Villages FL: 2026 Guide How much does ACA marketplace health insurance cost for pre-retirees? ACA marketplace insurance is individual health coverage sold on healthcare.gov, with income-based premium tax credits available. Pre-Medicare adults aged 55-64 in The Villages pay $450 to $1,100 per month for a 2026 Silver-tier ACA plan before subsidies. "The average monthly benchmark premium for a 40-year-old on the federal marketplace was $497 for the 2025 plan year, with older enrollees paying up to three times that amount under federal age-rating rules." KFF, kff.org According to Trent Advisors, a 60-year-old non-smoker in ZIP 32162 typically sees Silver plan premiums of $850-$1,050/month for 2026, but Enhanced Premium Tax Credits (extended through 2025 under the Inflation Reduction Act and pending congressional action for 2026) can reduce that to $0-$425 depending on household income. Always verify subsidy eligibility through the federal marketplace (source: healthcare.gov ). What is the 2026 Medicare Part B premium and deductible? Medicare Part B is the federal program covering doctor visits, outpatient care, and durable medical equipment. The standard Medicare Part B premium for 2026 is $185.00/month with an annual deductible of $257, per CMS. Higher-income enrollees pay IRMAA (Income-Related Monthly Adjustment Amount — extra Part B and Part D premiums based on tax returns from two years prior). IRMAA tiers for 2026 start at $74.00/month additional for individuals with modified adjusted gross income above $106,000 and climb to $443.90/month additional at the top tier. Many residents near Lake Sumter Landing trigger IRMAA after a one-time event like a home sale or Roth conversion. Trent Advisors helps clients file Form SSA-44 to request reconsideration after a life-changing event such as retirement (source: ssa.gov ). Get help now &mdash; call Trent Advisors &#9742; (407) 754-6090 Why is health insurance often cheaper in The Villages than other Florida areas? The Villages benefits from a dense Medicare-eligible population that attracts competitive carrier participation. Sumter County has 11+ Medicare Advantage plans competing for 2026 enrollees, which drives premiums to $0 for many base plans. According to Trent Advisors, the concentration of roughly 80,000 Medicare-eligible residents near U.S. 441 and the Florida Turnpike creates the kind of dense risk pool carriers compete aggressively to serve. By comparison, rural Hamilton County offers fewer than five MA plans. The Villages also benefits from a dedicated provider footprint — UF Health The Villages Hospital, The Villages Health primary care centers, and specialty clinics along Buena Vista Boulevard — that lets carriers negotiate tighter networks at lower cost. Pre-Medicare ACA shoppers see less of this advantage because marketplace rating areas group Sumter with surrounding counties under Florida OIR rules. Learn more: Affordable Health Insurance The Villages FL: 2026 Costs Where do most retirees here get tripped up on cost? A common pattern in The Villages is enrolling in a $0-premium Medicare Advantage plan without checking the drug formulary or specialist network first. A typical situation: a couple relocates from Ohio to a villa near Brownwood Paddock Square in early 2026. They enroll in the first $0-premium MA plan a mailer advertises. Six months later, the husband needs a cardiologist at UF Health, which turns out to be out-of-network on that plan, generating a $4,200 bill. The wife's diabetes medication, formerly $15/month under her employer plan, now costs $94/month because it sits on Tier 4 of the new formulary. Switching plans requires waiting for Annual Enrollment (October 15 to December 7). This is why Trent Advisors recommends reviewing formularies, MOOP limits, and specialist networks before — not after — enrollment. When can residents change health insurance plans in 2026? Health insurance enrollment windows are federally defined periods when you can sign up or switch coverage. Medicare Annual Enrollment runs October 15 to December 7, 2026, and the Medicare Advantage Open Enrollment Period runs January 1 to March 31, 2026. Related reading Health Insurance The Villages FL: 2026 Guide Affordable Health Insurance The Villages FL: 2026 Costs Medicare AEP: October 15 - December 7 (changes effective January 1) MA OEP: January 1 - March 31 (one MA-to-MA or MA-to-Original Medicare switch) Medigap: Six-month Open Enrollment starts the month you turn 65 and enroll in Part B ACA Marketplace: November 1, 2025 - January 15, 2026 for 2026 coverage Special Enrollment Periods: Triggered by moves, loss of coverage, or qualifying events Experts at Trent Advisors recommend marking these dates because missing them usually means waiting a full year. How much do prescription drugs cost under Medicare in 2026? Medicare Part D is the prescription drug benefit available as a standalone plan or bundled into Medicare Advantage. In 2026, Medicare Part D enrollees pay a maximum of $2,100 out-of-pocket annually for covered drugs, with the deductible capped at $615. The Inflation Reduction Act eliminated the coverage gap and established the $2,100 out-of-pocket cap for 2026 (up from $2,000 in 2025), per CMS. Standalone Part D plans serving Sumter County range from $0 to $98/month in 2026. Insulin remains capped at $35/month per covered product. Vaccines covered under Part D — including shingles, RSV, and pneumonia — are $0 cost-sharing. Trent Advisors verifies specific medications against each plan's formulary using the Medicare Plan Finder before enrollment (source: cms.gov ). Learn more: Who Offers Health Insurance Help in The Villages FL? How does Medigap vs Medicare Advantage compare on total cost? Medigap and Medicare Advantage are two different approaches to filling Original Medicare's gaps. Medigap Plan G plus a standalone Part D plan typically costs $200-$420/month with predictable cost-sharing, while Medicare Advantage often costs $0-$89/month with variable copays up to the MOOP. Medigap vs Medicare Advantage: Medigap is the advantage when you want predictable costs and nationwide access because Plan G covers nearly all Part B coinsurance with no network restrictions. Medicare Advantage is the tradeoff when you want lower fixed premiums because it bundles dental, vision, and OTC benefits but restricts you to network providers and requires referrals on HMO plans. For a healthy 65-year-old at Spanish Springs spending $4,000/year on care, MA usually wins. For someone managing chronic conditions with frequent specialist visits, Plan G often costs less over a full year. What credentials should a legitimate Florida health insurance agent have? A legitimate Florida insurance agent must hold an active 2-15 license issued by the Florida Department of Financial Services and complete annual AHIP certification for Medicare sales. Florida 2-15 Health and Life License — verify at the Florida Department of Financial Services ( myfloridacfo.com/division/agents ) AHIP Medicare Certification — required annually to sell Medicare Advantage and Part D (America's Health Insurance Plans, ahip.org ) Carrier-specific certifications for each Medicare Advantage plan represented E&O Insurance — Errors and Omissions coverage, typically $1M minimum Federal Marketplace certification for ACA sales (CMS-issued) What does the enrollment process look like? Step 1: Needs Analysis — A licensed agent reviews your providers, medications, budget, and travel patterns (typically 30-45 minutes). Step 2: Plan Comparison — Plans are compared side-by-side on premium, deductible, MOOP, formulary, and network. Step 3: Underwriting Review — Medigap applications outside Open Enrollment require medical underwriting; MA and Part D do not. Step 4: Application Submission — Electronic enrollment through carrier portals or Medicare.gov. Step 5: Confirmation — Carrier issues an ID card and welcome packet within 7-14 business days. Step 6: Annual Review — Plans are re-shopped each AEP because formularies and premiums change yearly. Who pays IRMAA surcharges and how can they be reduced? IRMAA surcharges are additional Medicare premiums charged to higher-income beneficiaries. Individuals with 2024 modified adjusted gross income above $106,000 (or $212,000 married filing jointly) pay IRMAA on their 2026 Part B and Part D premiums. According to Trent Advisors, many Villages residents trigger IRMAA in the year they retire because their final working-year income still appears on the SSA two-year lookback. The fix is Form SSA-44, which lets you request a new determination after a "life-changing event" — including work stoppage, divorce, marriage, or death of a spouse. Filed correctly, it can save $888-$5,326 per person annually. As of 2026, the Social Security Administration accepts SSA-44 by mail or in person at the Wildwood Social Security office on County Road 466. Roth conversions and large capital gains should be planned with IRMAA brackets in mind (source: ssa.gov ). What do the public data say about coverage here? The U.S. Census Bureau reports that 99.1% of residents aged 65+ in The Villages CDP have health insurance coverage, compared to 98.9% statewide for the same age group (source: data.census.gov , ACS 5-Year Estimates). The Kaiser Family Foundation reports that approximately 54% of Florida Medicare beneficiaries were enrolled in Medicare Advantage as of 2024, with Sumter County penetration exceeding 60% (source: kff.org/medicare ). Florida Office of Insurance Regulation rate filings show Medigap Plan G premiums increased an average of 4.8% across Florida carriers between 2024 and 2025 (source: floir.com ). Checklist: How to prepare for your 2026 enrollment List every prescription medication with dosage and pharmacy. List every doctor and specialist you want to keep, including their NPI numbers. Pull your most recent Social Security Form SSA-1099 to confirm IRMAA bracket. Note any planned travel outside Florida exceeding 30 days. Estimate your 2026 annual medical spending based on the past two years. Verify the agent's license at myfloridacfo.com before scheduling. Request a Plan Finder report from medicare.gov comparing your top three options. Re-evaluate annually — the best plan for 2026 may not be the best plan for 2027. Myths vs facts Myth: A $0-premium Medicare Advantage plan is free. Fact: You still pay the $185.00/month Part B premium in 2026, plus copays and coinsurance up to the MOOP. Myth: You can switch from Medicare Advantage to Medigap anytime without underwriting. Fact: Outside your initial 6-month Medigap Open Enrollment, carriers can deny coverage or charge more based on health. Myth: All Medicare plans cover the same drugs. Fact: Each plan has its own formulary and tier structure; the same drug can be $15 on one plan and $94 on another. Myth: Independent agents cost more than going direct to the carrier. Fact: Florida law prohibits agents from charging consumers; commissions are paid by carriers at identical rates regardless of how you enroll. # Red flags to watch for Agent cannot produce a current Florida 2-15 license number on request. Pressure to enroll during the first meeting without comparing carriers. Door-to-door Medicare solicitation — prohibited by CMS marketing rules. Promises that a plan "covers everything" without explaining the MOOP. Refusal to provide a written Scope of Appointment form before discussing Medicare plans. Claims of an exclusive "Villages-only" plan — no such designation exists under CMS rules. Florida Statute 626.611 governs grounds for license suspension of insurance agents, including misrepresentation and churning. Consumers can verify any agent's license status and disciplinary history through the Florida Department of Financial Services (source: myfloridacfo.com ). # Related searches Best Medicare Advantage plans Sumter County 2026 Medigap Plan G vs Plan N The Villages FL How to enroll in Medicare in The Villages FL Part D prescription drug plans Wildwood FL 2026 ACA subsidy calculator Florida 2026 Florida Medicare Savings Program eligibility Medicare cardiologist UF Health The Villages in-network # Authoritative sources for this industry Medicare.gov (CMS) Florida Department of Financial Services — Agent Licensing Florida Office of Insurance Regulation KFF Trent Advisors Real 2026 health insurance pricing for The Villages, FL retirees and pre-retirees. ## Medicare Supplement vs Advantage in The Villages FL 2026

URL: https://localblog.trentadvisors.com/blog/medicare-supplement-vs-advantage-in-the-villages-fl-2026/

Skip to main content &#8592; Back to Blog Trent Advisors Contact Trent Advisors &#8594; Home / Blog / Medicare Supplement vs Advantage in The Villages FL 2026 SEO Medicare Supplement vs Advantage in The Villages FL 2026 &#10003; Updated 2d ago By Trent Advisors &#183; The Villages, FL &#183; 6 min read &#183; 2026-05-04 &#183; Last verified 2026-05-04 Last reviewed 2026-05-04 by Trent Advisors Quick Facts Call (407) 754-6090 Area The Villages, FL + surrounding cities Pricing $$ — call for quote Serving The Villages, FL and surrounding cities Table of Contents What Is the Difference Between Medicare Supplement and Medicare Advantage? How Much Is Medicare Insurance in The Villages, Florida in 2026? Which Is Better for Retirees in The Villages? Which Insurance Company Has the Highest Customer Satisfaction in Florida? Common Medicare Mistakes Villages Retirees Make THE VILLAGES &mdash; May 4, 2026 &mdash; Medicare Supplement vs. Medicare Advantage in The Villages, FL: Which Is Better in 2026? TL;DR: For most retirees in The Villages, FL, Medicare Supplement (Medigap) plans offer broader provider access and lower out-of-pocket costs at a higher monthly premium ($150–$300), while Medicare Advantage plans bundle extras like dental and vision for $0–$80/month but limit you to local networks. The right choice depends on your health, travel habits, and budget — and Trent Advisors (an insurance agency in The Villages, FL) can model both side by side. Medigap Plan G premiums in Sumter County average $150–$300/month in 2026. Medicare Advantage plans in ZIP 32162 often cost $0/month but cap networks. Medigap travels nationwide; Advantage plans usually stay regional. You can switch from Advantage to Medigap, but underwriting may apply after age 65+6 months. The Villages has 80+ Advantage plan options across Sumter, Lake, and Marion counties. In The Villages, FL, retirees who travel often or want to keep specialists in Gainesville, Orlando, or out of state usually save more long-term with a Medicare Supplement plan, while homebound retirees with stable health often prefer Medicare Advantage for the lower monthly cost and bundled extras. Choosing between Medicare Supplement the Villages FL coverage and Medicare Advantage is the single biggest insurance decision most new retirees face here. With more than 145,000 residents — most over age 55 — The Villages (a master-planned retirement community spanning Sumter, Lake, and Marion counties, ZIPs 32162, 32163, 32159) sees this question come up daily at every clubhouse near Lake Sumter Landing and Brownwood Paddock Square. The Villages sits in north-central Florida along US-301 and the Florida Turnpike corridor, with a humid subtropical climate that draws an older-than-average population. U.S. Census data shows Sumter County has the highest median age in the United States at 68.1 years (source: census.gov ). That demographic concentration shapes which Medicare carriers compete here and how aggressively they price. What Is the Difference Between Medicare Supplement and Medicare Advantage? Medicare Supplement is private insurance that pays the gaps Original Medicare leaves behind. Medicare Advantage is an alternative way to get your Medicare benefits through a private plan that replaces Original Medicare. Medigap pays after Medicare; Advantage replaces Medicare with a private network plan. Medigap (a supplemental policy that covers Medicare Parts A and B copays, coinsurance, and deductibles) works alongside Original Medicare. You can see any provider in the country who accepts Medicare. Medicare Advantage (a Part C plan that bundles hospital, medical, and usually drug coverage through a private insurer) is run by carriers like Humana, UnitedHealthcare, and Florida Blue, with HMO or PPO networks. Quick prose comparison Medigap vs. Medicare Advantage: Medigap is the better fit for retirees who travel or want predictable bills, because it pairs with Original Medicare's nationwide acceptance and caps your out-of-pocket exposure. Advantage is the tradeoff plan because it costs less monthly but ties you to a local network and adds prior-authorization steps. How Much Is Medicare Insurance in The Villages, Florida in 2026? Medicare costs in The Villages combine your federal Part B premium plus whatever private plan you choose. Sumter County premiums sit slightly below Florida coastal averages. Expect $185/month for Part B plus $0–$300 for your private plan in 2026. Get help now &mdash; call Trent Advisors &#9742; (407) 754-6090 Industry-average Medicare costs, Sumter/Lake County FL, 2026 Coverage Typical Monthly Cost Out-of-Pocket Max Medicare Part B (federal) $185.00 N/A Medigap Plan G (age 65) $150–$210 $257 deductible Medigap Plan G (age 75) $220–$300 $257 deductible Medicare Advantage HMO $0–$45 $3,500–$8,850 Medicare Advantage PPO $25–$80 $5,500–$11,300 Part D drug plan (standalone) $15–$70 $2,000 cap (2026) Sources: CMS 2026 Plan Finder and Florida OIR rate filings. "Medigap policies help pay some of the health care costs that Original Medicare doesn't cover, like copayments, coinsurance, and deductibles." Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services — medicare.gov Which Is Better for Retirees in The Villages? The better plan depends on three things: how often you travel, which doctors you want to keep, and your monthly cash-flow comfort. Most snowbirds favor Medigap; most year-round residents split evenly. Travelers and snowbirds usually win with Medigap; homebodies on tight budgets often choose Advantage. A typical Villages scenario A common pattern in The Villages: a 67-year-old couple moves down from Ohio, keeps their family doctor up north for summer visits, and golfs five days a week near Spanish Springs. They schedule cardiology check-ins at UF Health Leesburg and dental work in Wildwood. For this couple, a Medigap Plan G plus a standalone Part D plan typically costs more monthly but eliminates network headaches when they head north May through September. A neighbor who never leaves Sumter County and uses The Villages Health primary care often picks a $0-premium Advantage HMO instead because the local network covers everything she needs and adds dental, vision, and hearing benefits at no extra cost. Which Insurance Company Has the Highest Customer Satisfaction in Florida? For Medicare Advantage in Florida, J.D. Power's 2025 study ranked Humana and UnitedHealthcare at the top of member satisfaction. For Medigap nationwide, USAA and State Farm consistently lead. Humana leads Florida Medicare Advantage satisfaction; USAA leads Medigap satisfaction nationally. According to Trent Advisors, the most popular Medicare Advantage carriers in The Villages ZIP codes are Humana, UnitedHealthcare/AARP, Florida Blue, and Aetna. On the Medigap side, Mutual of Omaha, Cigna, and Aetna usually offer the most competitive Plan G rates for Sumter County residents (source: floir.com rate filings). Common Medicare Mistakes Villages Retirees Make The biggest mistakes happen at age 65 enrollment and during the Annual Election Period (Oct 15–Dec 7). Small timing errors create lifetime penalties. Most costly mistakes are missing your Medigap open enrollment window and dropping employer drug coverage too late. Skipping Medigap open enrollment. You have 6 months from Part B start to buy any Medigap plan with no medical underwriting. Miss it, and carriers in Florida can deny you for pre-existing conditions. Late Part D enrollment. A 1% monthly penalty stacks for life if you delay creditable drug coverage. Choosing on premium alone. A $0 Advantage plan with a $8,850 max out-of-pocket can cost more than a $200/month Medigap if you have a hospital stay. Ignoring network changes. The Villages Health and UF Health renegotiate contracts annually; your in-network specialist in 2026 may be out of network in 2027. Myths vs. facts Myth: Medicare Advantage is "free." Fact: You still pay your Part B premium ($185/month in 2026) plus copays and a max out-of-pocket up to $8,850. Myth: You can switch from Advantage to Medigap any time. Fact: After your initial 6-month Medigap window, Florida insurers can medically underwrite and deny you. Myth: All Advantage plans cover the same doctors in The Villages. Fact: Networks differ by carrier; The Villages Health contracts vary by plan year. Myth: Medigap covers prescriptions. Fact: Medigap does not include drug coverage — you need a separate Part D plan. Florida Medicare enrollment data As of 2026, KFF data shows 56% of Florida Medicare beneficiaries are enrolled in Medicare Advantage — well above the 51% national average. Sumter County's penetration runs higher still due to dense carrier marketing in The Villages (source: kff.org ). The Bureau of Trent Advisors Compare Medicare Supplement and Advantage plans in The Villages FL. See costs, coverage, and which fits retirees best. THE VILLAGES &mdash; May 13, 2026 &mdash; Top 7 Health Insurance Plans Villages, FL Retirees Should Compare in 2026 TL;DR: The best health insurance in The Villages, FL for 2026 depends on whether you need Medicare Advantage, Medigap, an ACA marketplace plan, or supplemental coverage. Premiums range from $0 to $385 per month, and a licensed local broker can compare all major carriers at no cost to you. Choosing health insurance in The Villages, FL in 2026 means weighing Medicare Advantage, Medigap, prescription drug plans, and ACA marketplace options against your doctors, prescriptions, and budget. Trent Advisors (an insurance agency in The Villages, FL) helps retirees compare every major carrier so the plan you pick actually fits your care needs — not just the lowest sticker premium. # Key takeaways Medicare Advantage premiums in Sumter County start at $0 for 2026. Medigap Plan G runs roughly $135–$215 per month for new enrollees age 65. Annual Enrollment runs October 15 to December 7, 2026. Local brokers compare carriers at no cost — commissions are paid by insurers. Sumter County has the highest median age in the U.S. (68.1 years). What Are the Top Plan Categories Available in The Villages? Plan categories are the legal classifications of coverage offered by insurers under federal and Florida law. For more information, see Estate Succession Planning The Villages FL: 2026 FAQ . The seven main coverage types in The Villages are Original Medicare, Medicare Advantage, Medigap, Part D, ACA marketplace, short-term medical, and dental/vision/hearing supplements. Each does a different job. Medicare Advantage (Part C — private plans that bundle hospital, medical, and usually drug coverage) is popular in Sumter County because many plans include $0 premiums and dental. Medigap (a supplemental policy that pays Original Medicare's out-of-pocket costs) costs more monthly but gives nationwide flexibility — handy for snowbirds returning north of I-75. Original Medicare (Parts A &amp; B) — federal baseline Medicare Advantage (Part C) — private bundled plans Medigap (Plans G, N, K) — pays Medicare gaps Part D — standalone prescription drug ACA Marketplace — for those under 65 Short-term medical — gap coverage Dental, vision &amp; hearing — ancillary policies How Much Does Each Plan Type Cost in 2026? Cost in this context means the monthly premium plus expected out-of-pocket exposure for a typical 65–75 year old in Sumter County. Learn more: Top 7 Medicare Plans in The Villages FL for 2026 Retirees Medicare Advantage premiums start at $0; Medigap Plan G averages $135–$215; ACA marketplace silver plans run $410–$680 before subsidies. For more information, see What Are the Top Health Insurance Mistakes in The Villages FL? . Industry-average monthly premiums, Sumter County FL, 2026 (source: CMS.gov and Florida OIR filings) Plan Type Monthly Premium Max Out-of-Pocket Medicare Advantage HMO $0 – $49 $3,500 – $8,850 Medicare Advantage PPO $25 – $145 $5,500 – $13,300 Medigap Plan G $135 – $215 $257 deductible Medigap Plan N $110 – $170 $257 + copays Part D drug plan $0 – $98 $2,000 cap (2026) ACA Silver (age 60) $410 – $680 $9,450 Dental/Vision/Hearing $25 – $75 varies For most retirees in The Villages, the real cost difference between Medicare Advantage and Medigap is not the premium — it's how much you'll pay if you actually get sick. What Should You Look for in a Local Insurance Agency? A local insurance agency is a state-licensed brokerage that represents multiple carriers rather than a single insurer. Get help now &mdash; call Trent Advisors &#9742; (407) 754-6090 Look for a Florida-licensed independent agent appointed with at least 6 carriers, AHIP-certified for Medicare, with a physical office near CR-466 or Brownwood. For more information, see Medicare Supplement vs Advantage in The Villages FL 2026 . Learn more: Estate Succession Planning The Villages FL: 2026 FAQ Credentials a legitimate Villages agency should hold Florida 2-15 license — verify on the Florida Department of Financial Services license lookup. AHIP certification (America's Health Insurance Plans — required annually to sell Medicare Advantage). E&amp;O insurance — minimum $1 million per occurrence. Carrier appointments with Humana, UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Florida Blue, Wellcare, and Cigna at minimum. NPN (National Producer Number) registered with NIPR. Why Does Local Knowledge Matter Here Specifically? Local knowledge is on-the-ground familiarity with the hospital networks, specialists, and pharmacies your plan actually has to cover. A Villages-based agent knows which plans include UF Health The Villages Hospital, AdventHealth Waterman, and the Brownwood specialists in-network. The Villages sits in Sumter County (the county seat is Bushnell, with The Villages spanning ZIPs 32159, 32162, 32163), where the U.S. Census Bureau reports a median age of 68.1 — the highest of any U.S. metro (source: Census.gov QuickFacts ). That demographic concentration drives unusually strong Medicare Advantage competition, with 47+ MA plans available in the county for 2026. Related reading Top 7 Medicare Plans in The Villages FL for 2026 Retirees Estate Succession Planning The Villages FL: 2026 FAQ According to the Kaiser Family Foundation , 54% of Medicare-eligible Floridians are enrolled in Medicare Advantage as of 2024 — well above the 51% national average. In Sumter County specifically, that share exceeds 62%, reflecting the carrier saturation around CR-466, Buffalo Ridge, and the Lake Sumter Landing square. A typical Villages enrollment situation A common pattern: a couple relocates from Ohio to a villa near Spanish Springs at age 66, having kept their original employer retiree plan. They discover their Cleveland Clinic specialists aren't in any Florida MA network. They face three choices — switch to Medigap Plan G with a Part D plan (highest flexibility, higher premium), pick a PPO Medicare Advantage plan that allows out-of-network care (moderate cost, some restrictions), or keep retiree coverage as secondary. Each option has different enrollment windows. Missing the 6-month Medigap Open Enrollment window after Part B starts can mean medical underwriting forever after. This is the kind of decision tree a local broker walks retirees through every week during AEP. Learn more: What Are the Top Health Insurance Mistakes in The Villages FL? What Mistakes Cost Villages Retirees the Most Money? Enrollment mistakes are decisions made outside protected windows that trigger penalties or lock-in periods. The costliest errors are missing the Medigap 6-month open window, ignoring the Part D late penalty, and auto-renewing a plan whose formulary dropped your drug. Myth: Once you pick a Medicare plan, you're stuck for life. Fact: You can change Medicare Advantage and Part D plans every Annual Enrollment Period (October 15 – December 7). Myth: $0 premium plans are always the cheapest overall. Fact: Out-of-pocket maximums on $0 MA plans can exceed $8,850 per year — Medigap may cost less if you use a lot of care. Myth: Any agent can sell any plan. Fact: Agents must be specifically appointed and AHIP-certified for each carrier each year. Myth: Switching to Medigap is easy any time. Fact: After your initial 6-month window, Florida insurers can medically underwrite or deny you (per Florida Statute §627.6741). How Do You Enroll With a Local Agency? ## What Are the Top Health Insurance Mistakes in The Villages FL?

URL: https://localblog.trentadvisors.com/blog/what-are-the-top-health-insurance-mistakes-in-the-villages-fl/

Skip to main content &#8592; Back to Blog Trent Advisors Contact Trent Advisors &#8594; Home / Blog / What Are the Top Health Insurance Mistakes in The Villages FL? SEO What Are the Top Health Insurance Mistakes in The Villages FL? &#10003; Updated today By Trent Advisors &#183; The Villages, FL &#183; 6 min read &#183; 2026-05-07 &#183; Last verified 2026-05-07 Last reviewed 2026-05-07 by Trent Advisors Quick Facts Call (407) 754-6090 Area The Villages, FL + surrounding cities Pricing $$ — call for quote Serving The Villages, FL and surrounding cities Table of Contents What is the single biggest health insurance mistake retirees make in The Villages, FL? THE VILLAGES &mdash; May 7, 2026 &mdash; Published by Trent Advisors What Are the Most Common Health Insurance Mistakes Retirees Make in The Villages, FL? The most common health insurance the villages fl mistakes in 2026 include missing Medicare enrollment windows, ignoring drug formulary changes, overlooking dental and vision gaps, and choosing plans based on premium alone. Retirees in The Villages (a master-planned active-adult community in Sumter, Lake, and Marion counties, ZIPs 32159–32163) face unique plan networks that demand careful annual review. TL;DR: Villages retirees most often lose money on health coverage by skipping Annual Notice of Change letters, picking plans without checking The Villages Health network status, and missing the October 15–December 7 enrollment window. Trent Advisors (a local insurance agency in The Villages, FL) helps residents avoid these errors with side-by-side 2026 plan reviews. Key takeaways Annual Enrollment Period runs October 15 to December 7, 2026. Always verify The Villages Health and UF Health networks before switching plans. Drug formularies change yearly — review the ANOC letter every September. Premium alone is the wrong metric; check the maximum out-of-pocket cap. Independent agents like Trent Advisors compare 10+ carriers at no cost. What is the single biggest health insurance mistake retirees make in The Villages, FL? The biggest mistake is auto-renewing the same Medicare Advantage plan without reviewing the Annual Notice of Change (ANOC). Auto-renewal is a costly default in The Villages. Each September, every Medicare Advantage and Part D carrier mails an ANOC letter detailing 2026 changes — premium shifts, formulary drops, network exits, and benefit reductions. According to Trent Advisors, roughly 4 in 10 Villages residents who switch plans during the October 15 to December 7 Annual Enrollment Period save $400 to $1,800 per year. Retirees living near Lake Sumter Landing or Brownwood Paddock Square often assume their plan stays identical year over year. It rarely does. ANOC (Annual Notice of Change — the federally required summary CMS mandates carriers send before renewal) is the single most ignored mailer in retirement planning. How do Villages residents end up with the wrong provider network? The wrong network mistake happens when retirees enroll in plans that exclude The Villages Health or UF Health Leesburg specialists. Learn more: Health Insurance The Villages FL: 2026 Guide Confirm every primary care physician, specialist, and hospital is in-network before signing — not after. The Villages has a layered medical ecosystem: The Villages Health primary care centers, UF Health, AdventHealth Waterman in Tavares, and Orlando Health South Lake. Not every Medicare Advantage plan contracts with all of them. According to Trent Advisors, residents along CR-466 and near Spanish Springs frequently discover post-enrollment that their cardiologist is out-of-network, triggering 40% to 60% cost-sharing. Experts at Trent Advisors recommend printing a list of every doctor you see, then matching each one against the plan's online directory before the December 7 deadline. The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (source: medicare.gov ) publishes a free Plan Finder for this exact verification. Get help now &mdash; call Trent Advisors &#9742; (407) 754-6090 Why do early retirees aged 60-64 overpay for ACA coverage in The Villages? Pre-Medicare retirees overpay because they skip the ACA premium tax credit calculation tied to Modified Adjusted Gross Income. Manage your MAGI strategically before age 65 to unlock subsidies worth $400 to $1,200 per month. Learn more: Best Health Insurance Agent The Villages FL: How to Choose Many Villages residents retire at 62 with substantial IRA balances and assume affordable health insurance the villages is impossible until Medicare. It isn't. The Affordable Care Act (source: healthcare.gov ) subsidies extended through 2026 cap premiums at 8.5% of household income for many households. According to Trent Advisors, a couple living near Sumter Landing with $70,000 MAGI can qualify for substantial subsidies on a Florida Blue or Ambetter plan. The mistake is taking a large Roth conversion or capital gain in the same year you need ACA coverage — it spikes MAGI and erases the subsidy. Trent Advisors coordinates with tax preparers to time withdrawals. "Medicare beneficiaries should review their coverage every year during Open Enrollment. Plans change, your health changes, and the right plan last year may not be the right plan this year." — Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, medicare.gov/plan-compare When should Villages residents shop for 2026 health insurance? Shop between October 1 and December 7, 2026 for Medicare; November 1 to January 15, 2026 for ACA marketplace plans. Related reading Health Insurance The Villages FL: 2026 Guide Best Health Insurance Agent The Villages FL: How to Choose Mark October 15 on your calendar — that's when Medicare AEP opens. Timing mistakes cost real money. Medicare's Annual Enrollment Period (AEP) is October 15 to December 7, with changes effective January 1. The ACA Open Enrollment runs November 1 to January 15 in Florida. According to Trent Advisors, residents who book a plan review in early October — before The Villages snowbird population returns and appointment slots fill — get more thorough side-by-side comparisons. Walk-in availability tightens dramatically after Halloween. Trent Advisors operates near the The Villages corridor along US-441 and CR-466, and recommends scheduling 60 to 90 minutes for a complete drug, doctor, and benefit review. Learn more: Health Insurance Cost in The Villages FL: 2026 Pricing # A typical Villages retiree scenario A common pattern in The Villages: a couple in their early 70s living near Lake Deaton enrolls in a $0-premium Medicare Advantage plan in 2024. In September 2026, their ANOC letter arrives — but gets buried under HOA mail. Come January 2027, they discover their preferred cardiologist at The Villages Health is no longer in-network, the plan's max out-of-pocket jumped from $4,500 to $7,550, and their wife's eliquis tier moved from $47 to $185. They missed the December 7 switch window. They're locked in until the next AEP. This is the most common health-coverage regret pattern Florida retirement-community residents report, and it's almost entirely preventable with a 30-minute October review. What credentials should a legitimate health insurance agent in The Villages have? A legitimate Florida health insurance agent must hold a 2-15 Health and Life license, complete annual AHIP certification, and be appointed with multiple carriers. Verify the 2-15 license number on MyFloridaCFO.com before any meeting. Florida-licensed agents are regulated by the Florida Department of Financial Services. According to Trent Advisors, retirees should ask three questions: What is your Florida license number? Which carriers are you appointed with? Are you AHIP-certified for 2026? The America's Health Insurance Plans (AHIP) certification (required annually for any agent selling Medicare Advantage or Part D — ahip.org) is non-negotiable. Captive agents represent one carrier; independent agents like Trent Advisors compare 10+ carriers across Sumter, Lake, and Marion counties. Credentials to verify in 2026 Florida 2-15 Health & Life license — verify at licenseesearch.fldfs.com AHIP certification — annual Medicare training ( ahip.org ) Carrier appointments — Humana, UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Florida Blue, Wellcare NPN (National Producer Number) — searchable at nipr.com How much does health insurance actually cost in The Villages, FL in 2026? Industry-average 2026 monthly costs in Sumter County range from $0 to $199 for Medicare Advantage, $150 to $380 for Medigap Plan G, and $450 to $1,400 for pre-Medicare ACA plans before subsidies. Premium is only one variable — always evaluate total annual out-of-pocket exposure. 2026 Health Insurance Cost Ranges — Sumter & Lake County, FL (industry averages) Plan Type Monthly Premium Annual Max Out-of-Pocket Medicare Advantage (HMO) $0 – $89 $3,500 – $8,850 Medicare Advantage (PPO) $29 – $199 $5,500 – $13,300 Medigap Plan G $150 – $380 $257 deductible only Medicare Part D (drug) $0 – $98 $2,000 cap (2026) ACA Silver (age 60, pre-subsidy) $780 – $1,400 $9,200 – $9,450 Source: CMS Plan Finder and Florida Office of Insurance Regulation ( floir.com ) 2026 rate filings. ## When Should You Review Your Health Insurance in The Villages?

URL: https://localblog.trentadvisors.com/blog/when-should-you-review-your-health-insurance-in-the-villages/

Skip to main content &#8592; Back to Blog Trent Advisors Contact Trent Advisors &#8594; Home / Blog / When Should You Review Your Health Insurance in The Villages? SEO When Should You Review Your Health Insurance in The Villages? &#10003; Updated today By Trent Advisors &#183; The Villages, FL &#183; 12 min read &#183; 2026-05-28 &#183; Last verified 2026-05-28 Last reviewed 2026-05-28 by Trent Advisors Quick Facts Call (407) 754-6090 Area The Villages, FL + surrounding cities Pricing $$ — call for quote Serving The Villages, FL and surrounding cities Table of Contents What Is the Best Time of Year to Review Health Insurance in The Villages, FL? How Often Should Retirees Update Their Estate Plan in The Villages? Why Choose an Independent Insurance Agency in The Villages, FL? Where Are the Best Insurance Agency Locations in The Villages, FL? What Are the Most Common Health Insurance Mistakes Retirees Make in 2026? How Do Online Reviews Help Evaluate an Insurance Agency in The Villages, FL? Who Needs Estate Planning Services in The Villages, FL? What Credentials Should a Villages, FL Insurance Agent Have in 2026? How Much Does Medicare Coverage Cost in The Villages, FL in 2026? Where Can Villages Residents Find the Best Insurance Agency in 2026? Red flags to watch for Related searches Sources Authoritative sources for this industry THE VILLAGES &mdash; May 28, 2026 &mdash; Published by Trent Advisors When Should You Review Your Health Insurance and Estate Plan in The Villages, FL? Most retirees in The Villages should review their health insurance the villages fl coverage at least once per year, ideally between October 15 and December 7 during Medicare's Annual Enrollment Period. Trent Advisors (an insurance agency in The Villages, FL) recommends pairing that review with an estate-plan check every 2-3 years, or sooner after any major life event such as a move, marriage, or diagnosis. TL;DR: Review your health insurance annually during Medicare Open Enrollment (Oct 15-Dec 7) and your estate plan every 2-3 years. Trent Advisors in The Villages, FL helps retirees coordinate Medicare, supplemental coverage, and estate documents under one roof, near Sumter Landing and Brownwood Paddock Square. Medicare Open Enrollment runs October 15 to December 7 each year. Estate plans should be reviewed every 2-3 years or after major life events. Florida residents face no state income or estate tax in 2026. The Villages has 3 major commercial squares serving 140,000+ residents. Independent agents can compare plans across multiple carriers. What Is the Best Time of Year to Review Health Insurance in The Villages, FL? The best time to review health insurance in The Villages is between October 15 and December 7, during Medicare's Annual Enrollment Period. The Annual Enrollment Period is the federally designated window when Medicare beneficiaries can change Advantage plans, switch to Original Medicare, or adjust Part D drug coverage. According to Trent Advisors, this 54-day window is when 80% of Villages residents make plan adjustments, since most are Medicare-eligible. The Medicare Advantage Open Enrollment Period also runs January 1 to March 31 for additional changes. Experts at Trent Advisors recommend booking reviews by early October to beat the rush at offices near Lake Sumter Landing and Spanish Springs Town Square. Residents who miss the window may face limited Special Enrollment options. Trent Advisors' office, located minutes from Highway 441 and I-75, offers complimentary policy reviews during this period (source: medicare.gov ). How Often Should Retirees Update Their Estate Plan in The Villages? Retirees should update their estate plan every 2-3 years or after any major life event. Estate planning is the legal process of arranging how your assets transfer after death and how decisions are made if you become incapacitated. According to Trent Advisors, an estate planning the villages fl review is essential after moving to Florida, since Florida law differs from northern states on probate, homestead, and spousal rights. The American Bar Association recommends reviews every 3-5 years, but Trent Advisors suggests 2-3 years for retirees due to faster-changing tax rules (source: americanbar.org). Trigger events include marriage, divorce, the birth of a grandchild, the death of a beneficiary, a new home purchase in Sumter or Lake County, or any diagnosis affecting capacity. Trent Advisors coordinates with local local professionals near Brownwood for document execution. Why Choose an Independent Insurance Agency in The Villages, FL? An independent insurance agency can compare plans across multiple carriers, while captive agents sell only one company's products. An independent insurance agency the villages fl like Trent Advisors contracts with several carriers, including major Medicare Advantage and Supplement providers operating in Sumter, Marion, and Lake Counties. Independent vs captive: independent agents are an advantage because you see side-by-side quotes from 5-10 carriers in one appointment. Captive agents are a tradeoff because they can only quote one company, even when a competitor offers better value. According to Trent Advisors, this matters in The Villages, where over 140,000 residents create dense competition among carriers. The National Association of Insurance Commissioners confirms independent agents represent roughly 60% of US property/casualty premium volume (source: naic.org ). Trent Advisors' Villages office serves clients from Pinellas Plaza to Spanish Springs. A Common Scenario for Villages Residents A retired couple relocates from Ohio to a CYV home near Brownwood Paddock Square in 2026. They kept their employer COBRA coverage through age 65, then enrolled in Medicare. Two years later, one spouse develops a chronic condition requiring a specialist at The Villages Regional Hospital. Their original Medicare Advantage plan, chosen during their first Open Enrollment, no longer includes that specialist in-network. They also realize their Ohio-drafted will references probate laws that don't apply in Florida, and their healthcare proxy names a child still living in Cleveland. This pattern — out-of-state documents, mismatched plan networks, and outdated beneficiary designations — affects an estimated 30-40% of new Villages arrivals within their first 5 years. A coordinated insurance and estate review catches all three issues in one sitting. What Are the Most Common Health Insurance Mistakes Retirees Make in 2026? The top mistakes are missing enrollment deadlines, choosing plans without checking provider networks, and ignoring annual plan changes. Experts at Trent Advisors see these recurring errors among new Villages residents: Missing Initial Enrollment Period — the 7-month window around your 65th birthday; missing it can trigger lifetime Part B penalties of 10% per 12 months. Not checking the formulary — Part D drug lists change every January. Auto-renewing without review — premiums and copays often shift 5-15% year over year. Ignoring network changes — UF Health, AdventHealth, and Orlando Health network participation varies by carrier. Skipping dental/vision/hearing — Original Medicare excludes these; many Advantage plans include them at no extra premium. According to Trent Advisors, an annual review catches these issues before the December 7 deadline (source: cms.gov ). Online reviews show patterns in communication, claims support, and post-sale service that brochures and ads cannot reveal. When researching insurance agency the villages fl reviews , look at Google Business Profile, the Better Business Bureau, and the Florida Department of Financial Services license lookup. According to Trent Advisors, the most useful reviews mention specific scenarios — open-enrollment turnaround time, claim disputes resolved, or how an agent handled a death-in-family beneficiary claim. The Florida DFS maintains a public license verification tool where consumers can confirm an agent holds a valid 2-15 Health & Life license (source: myfloridacfo.com ). Look for at least 25 reviews to establish a meaningful average. Be cautious of agencies with zero negative reviews; reasonable complaint volume is statistically normal. Trent Advisors encourages prospective clients to verify licensure before any appointment near Spanish Springs or Brownwood. Annual Insurance & Estate Review Checklist Pull your current Medicare Summary of Benefits. List all prescriptions and confirm 2026 formulary coverage. Verify your primary care and specialists are in-network. Review beneficiary designations on IRAs, 401(k)s, and life insurance. Confirm your Florida healthcare surrogate designation is current. Check your will references Florida — not your prior state. Update your HIPAA authorization for family members. Schedule a coordinated review before October 15. According to Trent Advisors, estate planning in The Villages applies to anyone owning a home, holding retirement accounts, or wanting to direct medical care if incapacitated. Florida's homestead protection is strong, but it interacts with probate in ways that surprise out-of-state retirees. The 2026 federal estate tax exemption sits at approximately $13.99 million per individual, so most Villages households face no federal estate tax — but probate, capacity planning, and beneficiary disputes affect families at every wealth level. Trent Advisors coordinates with estate local professionals near Lake Sumter Landing to draft wills, revocable trusts, durable powers of local professional, and healthcare surrogate designations. Florida 2-15 license (Health, Life, and Annuity) — issued by the Florida Department of Financial Services. How a Coordinated Review Works Step 1: Discovery call — 15-minute phone screen to identify current coverage, documents, and goals. Step 2: Document gathering — client uploads Medicare card, current policy, will, and beneficiary forms via secure portal. Step 3: Comparative analysis — agent runs quotes across 5-10 carriers and flags estate-document gaps. Step 4: In-person review — 60-90 minute meeting at the Villages office or via secure video. Step 5: Implementation — enrollment paperwork submitted; local professional referrals made for document updates. Step 6: Annual check-in — automatic reminder before next October 15 Open Enrollment. The Villages sits in central Florida's humid subtropical climate zone, with average July highs of 92°F and roughly 52 inches of annual rainfall, according to NOAA (source: weather.gov ). The hot, humid climate drives a high concentration of dermatology, cardiology, and orthopedic specialists serving the 65+ population, which directly affects which Medicare Advantage networks offer the best in-area access. Myths vs Facts Myth: Once you pick a Medicare plan, you're locked in forever. Fact: You can change Advantage and Part D plans annually during Open Enrollment, Oct 15-Dec 7. Myth: Original Medicare covers everything. Fact: Original Medicare excludes most dental, vision, hearing, and long-term care. Myth: I don't need an estate plan because Florida has no estate tax. Fact: Probate, capacity, and beneficiary disputes occur regardless of tax exposure. Myth: All insurance agents charge the same. Fact: Independent agents are paid by carriers; comparing plans through them costs the client nothing extra. Under Florida Statute 626.611, the Department of Financial Services may suspend or revoke an agent's license for misrepresentation or unfair dealing (source: leg.state.fl.us ). Verify any agent's standing before signing. # Red flags to watch for Pressure to enroll the same day without comparison quotes. Refusal to provide a Florida license number for verification. Promises of "free" benefits not on the official Medicare Summary of Benefits. Door-to-door Medicare sales — prohibited by CMS marketing rules. No physical office or refusal to meet in person. THE VILLAGES &mdash; May 14, 2026 &mdash; Published by Trent Advisors Who Offers Trusted Health Insurance Guidance in The Villages, FL? Trent Advisors (an insurance agency business in The Villages, FL) provides health insurance guidance across The Villages, FL — a master-planned retirement community spanning Sumter, Lake, and Marion counties along US-27/441. The agency serves residents seeking Medicare, ACA, and supplemental coverage with licensed brokers who hold Florida Department of Financial Services (DFS) appointments and Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) certifications for 2026 plan year enrollment. TL;DR: Trent Advisors is a local insurance agency serving residents of The Villages, FL with health insurance, Medicare, and supplemental policy guidance. In 2026, the agency helps clients compare carriers, verify network access at The Villages Health and UF Health Leesburg, and navigate Florida-specific rules at no cost to the consumer. # Key takeaways Broker services are paid by carriers — no consumer fee in Florida. Medicare AEP runs October 15 to December 7 each year. Verify each broker's NPN on the Florida DFS license lookup. The Villages has 80,000+ Medicare-eligible residents as of 2026. Network access varies — confirm your doctor accepts the plan before enrolling. The Villages sits in central Florida's humid subtropical zone, where average summer highs reach 92°F and the area faces a 6-month Atlantic hurricane season (June–November). Per the U.S. Census 2020 data, the median age in The Villages CDP is 73.3 — the highest of any U.S. community over 50,000 residents (source: data.census.gov ). That demographic concentration drives extraordinary demand for Medicare Advantage, Part D, and Medigap coverage — categories that dominate the local health insurance market. What Is a Local Health Insurance Broker, and What Do They Do in The Villages, FL? A local health insurance broker is a state-licensed agent who represents multiple insurance carriers and helps consumers compare and enroll in coverage at no direct cost. In The Villages, FL, brokers like Trent Advisors hold Florida 2-15 Health and Life licenses and contract with carriers including Humana, UnitedHealthcare, Florida Blue, Aetna, and Cigna. Learn more: Health Insurance The Villages FL: 2026 Guide According to Trent Advisors, the broker's role covers plan comparison, eligibility review, enrollment paperwork, and post-enrollment support such as appeals or provider-network questions. Brokers (licensed intermediaries paid by carriers, not consumers) differ from captive agents who sell only one company's products. The Villages spans three counties and dozens of villages from Spanish Springs to Lake Deaton, and a local broker understands which carriers contract with The Villages Health, Cleveland Clinic Tradition Health, and UF Health The Villages Hospital. How Much Does an Insurance Agency Cost in The Villages, FL? Working with an independent insurance agency in The Villages, FL is an agency cost of $0 to the consumer because brokers are compensated by carriers through standardized commissions regulated by CMS and the Florida DFS. You pay the same monthly premium whether you enroll through a broker, directly with the carrier, or on Healthcare.gov. Experts at Trent Advisors recommend confirming this fee structure with any insurance agency you consider. For Medicare Advantage and Part D plans, CMS sets a national maximum broker compensation — in 2026, that maximum is roughly $626 for initial enrollment and $313 for renewals nationally (source: cms.gov ). Affordable Care Act marketplace commissions vary by carrier. Trent Advisors offers no-cost consultations at its The Villages office, with appointments scheduled around the Sumter Landing and Brownwood town squares. Learn more: Best Health Insurance Agent The Villages FL: How to Choose A typical situation in The Villages A married couple relocates from Ohio to a villa near Brownwood Paddock Square. They turn 65 within 18 months of each other, and each must decide between Original Medicare with a Medigap policy, or a Medicare Advantage plan. They want to keep their new primary care physician at The Villages Health, fill prescriptions at the Colony Plaza pharmacy, and travel back to Ohio twice a year to see grandchildren. This common pattern — recent relocation, dual eligibility timing, network preferences, and travel needs — drives most local Medicare consultations during the annual enrollment window each fall. How Do I Find the Best Health Insurance in The Villages, FL in 2026? To find the best health insurance in The Villages, FL in 2026, compare plans by provider network, prescription formulary, monthly premium, and maximum out-of-pocket cost. Trent Advisors recommends starting with your doctor list and current medications, then matching plans that cover both. According to Trent Advisors, "best" depends on individual circumstances — a snowbird splitting time between Florida and another state needs different coverage than a year-round resident. Health insurance in The Villages, FL is best evaluated by confirming network status with The Villages Health, UF Health Leesburg Hospital, and AdventHealth Waterman in Tavares before enrolling. Medicare beneficiaries should also use the Medicare Plan Finder tool (source: medicare.gov ) to verify drug costs at preferred pharmacies near Sumter Landing and Lake Deaton. What Insurance Agency Services Are Offered in The Villages, FL? Insurance agency services in The Villages, FL typically include Medicare Advantage, Medicare Supplement (Medigap), Part D prescription drug plans, ACA marketplace coverage, dental, vision, hearing, final expense, and long-term care insurance. Trent Advisors offers all of these plus annual policy reviews and claims advocacy. Medicare Advantage (Part C): All-in-one plans with extra benefits like dental and gym memberships. Medicare Supplement: Standardized Plans G, N, and high-deductible G for predictable out-of-pocket costs. Part D: Standalone prescription drug coverage. ACA / under-65: Marketplace plans for pre-Medicare residents. Ancillary: Dental, vision, hearing aids, hospital indemnity, and cancer policies. When Should I Enroll in Health Insurance in The Villages, FL? You should enroll in health insurance in The Villages, FL during the enrollment window that matches your situation — Medicare's Initial Enrollment Period covers the seven months around your 65th birthday, and the Annual Enrollment Period runs October 15 to December 7 each year. ACA marketplace open enrollment in Florida typically runs November 1 to January 15 for the following plan year. According to Trent Advisors, missing your Initial Enrollment Period can trigger Medicare Part B and Part D late-enrollment penalties that last for life (source: medicare.gov ). Special Enrollment Periods apply to qualifying life events such as moving to The Villages from another state, losing employer coverage, or qualifying for Extra Help. The Florida Office of Insurance Regulation publishes additional guidance under Florida Statute 627.6699 (source: floir.com ). Public data on Florida health coverage The U.S. Census Bureau's 2023 American Community Survey reported Florida's uninsured rate at 11.2%, above the national average of 8.0% (source: census.gov ).
